'''Seib Nod''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Lorrdian/Legends|Lorrdian]] member of the [[Sisterhood of the Beatific Countenance]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Lorrd/Legends|Lorrd]]. A lifelong member, she grew bored of her cloistered lifestyle and opted for a life of crime instead. Stealing valuable artifacts from her temple, she fled Lorrd and went to the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where she intended to sell the stolen items. She found a buyer in [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeologist]] [[Artuo Pratuhr]].

Pratuhr and his assistant, [[Zey Nep]], met with Nod at the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. The archaeologist was initially wary of Nod's intentions, but the Lorrdian was able to convince him that she was acting on behalf of the Sisterhood in selling the items. Once he had handed over his money, she decided to double-cross him and left with both the items and the [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. She was later apprehended by Pratuhr and Nep, and was given a long sentence in [[CoCo Penitentiary]].

==Biography==
A [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Near-Human]] [[Lorrdian/Legends|Lorrdian]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Lorrd/Legends|Lorrd]], Seib Nod was a lifelong member of the [[Sisterhood of the Beatific Countenance]]. As such, she was forced to live in a cloistered fashion, which soon bored her. With no funds whatsoever, she realized that in order to start a new life for herself, she would have to procure them from somewhere. Not caring how she went about it, Nod stole several valuable artifacts from her temple, and departed Lorrd post haste, for the galactic capital of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. There, she continued to wear her religious fatigues to mask her criminal intentions<ref name="db" /> and became a participant in Coruscant's party scene.<ref name="Rogues">{{InsiderCite|136|Rogues Gallery|Rogues Gallery: A Night at the Outlander Club}}</ref>

[[File:SeibNodOutlanderClub-AOTC.png|thumb|300px|Nod walks inside the Outlander Club.]]
Intending to pawn the stolen relics, she was contacted by [[Artuo Pratuhr]], an archeologist who wanted to buy them for the [[Galactic Museum/Legends|Galactic Museum]]. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="date">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of {{Film|II}} to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref> Nod met with Pratuhr and his assistant, [[Zey Nep]], at the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]],<ref name="db" /> a [[Nightclub/Legends|nightclub]] and [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] hall in Coruscant's [[Uscru Entertainment District/Legends|Uscru Entertainment District]],<ref name="CSWE2">{{CSWECite|II|431|Outlander Club}}</ref> at the height of the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]].<ref name="aotc">{{Film|II}}</ref> While at the Outlander Club, the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] entered, searching for the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]]. Nod walked behind the two as they split up. While Skywalker searched the club for the bounty hunter, she walked past two Humans. When Kenobi [[Dismemberment/Legends|disarmed]] Wesell, Nod walked near the club's bar.<ref name="aotc" /> Initially, the archeologist was suspicious of Nod's intentions, but she claimed that the Sisterhood was selling the items to raise desperately needed funds. The clandestine nature of the sale was to conceal the fact that the Sisterhood was in such financial trouble that it needed to sell the relics in the first place.<ref name="db" />

Pratuhr agreed to the deal, but the criminal atmosphere of [[Galactic City/Legends|Galactic City]] caused Nod to make a snap judgment—drawing a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] on the archeologist, she took his money and fled the Outlander Club for an outbound flight.<ref name="db" /> A few [[Local day/Legends|days]] later,<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|386|Nod, Seib}}</ref> while waiting at the dock, Nod was confronted by Pratuhr, who caught up to her. She did not feel threatened, however—she was armed, he was not. What Nod had not accounted for was the presence of Nep, who, standing behind her and disguised as a [[Refugee/Legends|refugee]], had her own blaster trained on her. Realizing that her plans were thwarted, she returned the money. Pratuhr then took her to the local authorities and returned the relics to the Sisterhood. Nod was then incarcerated in [[CoCo Penitentiary]] for a considerable amount of [[Time/Legends|time]], during which she was pardoned by the Sisterhood for her crimes.<ref name="db" />

==Personality and traits==
Although the Sisterhood of Beatific Countenance required lifelong servitude,<ref name="db" /> the withdrawn<ref name="Insider" /> Seib Nod did not find her servitude spiritually fulfilling in the slightest, and so, due to her boredom, sought out more material possessions. She was desperate enough to break free of her situation by turning to crime as a way of raising money for herself. Once she had committed one criminal act, she was prone to lying to others, and she found herself so changed by her one act that she felt free to perform another. When she was jailed, she was at such a point that the forgiveness of her fellow Sisters meant nothing to her. Nod had [[Color/Legends|light]] skin, and as with all members of the Sisterhood, Nod had scripture [[Tattoo/Legends|tattooed]] on her body, including distinctive facial patterns.<ref name="db" /> During her visit to the Outlander Club<ref name="aotc" /> in 22 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="date" /> Nod was a bachelorette.<ref name="Ladies">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/explore/attack/2002/06/imageattack20020621.html|series=Image Attack|text=Ladies of the Outlander}}</ref>

==Equipment==
While traditionally members of the Sisterhood wore their robes and masks outside their temple to hide their skin, Nod maintained her religious garb in order to hide her [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] nature.<ref name="db" /> Nod was distinguished by the<ref name="CSWE" /> mysterious<ref name="FansSetTheFacts">{{SWArchive|url=databank/updates/news20080321.html|text=What's the Story: The Fans Set the Facts}}</ref> blood-red ankle-length [[Clothing/Legends|robes]] she wore. The robes were attached to a unique facial screen that completely hid her face. Both the robes and the tattoos were obvious symbols of her membership in the Sisterhood.<ref name="CSWE" /> During her deal with Pratuhr, she pulled a blaster on him.<ref name="db" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:SeibNob.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Seib Nod was the subject of a What's the Story? submission.]]
Seib Nod first appeared in the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']],<ref name="aotc" /> which released on [[May 16]], [[2002]].<ref name="Year">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref> The Outlander Club scenes including Nod, who was portrayed by uncredited extra,<ref name="aotc" /> were shot at [[Fox Studios, Sydney]] on [[July 17]], [[2000]].<ref name="Mythmaking">''[[Mythmaking: Behind the Scenes of Attack of the Clones]]''</ref> Nod was first identified in an Episode II [[Depth Commentary]] available on [[StarWars.com]] that could be accessed through the DVD-ROM home release of ''Attack of the Clones''<ref name="Depth">{{Film|II}} DVD-ROM Depth Commentary</ref> on [[November 12]], 2002.<ref name="DVD">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/news/2002/08/news20020801.html|text=Episode II On DVD & VHS}}</ref> Spelled backwards, Seib Nod is [[Don Bies]], a special-effects artist for ''Attack of the Clones'' who played Artuo Pratuhr.<ref name="Insider">{{InsiderCite|75|Who's Who in the Outlander Club: It's Ladies' Night}}</ref>

[[Trisha Biggar]]'s [[October 11]], [[2005]]<ref name="DressingDate">{{WebCite|url=https://www.waterstones.com/book/dressing-a-galaxy-part-1/trisha-biggar/george-lucas/9780810959644|text=Dressing a Galaxy: Part 1: The Costumes of Star Wars (Hardback)|work=[[Wikipedia:Waterstones|Waterstones]]|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/mxQvt|archivedate=2022-04-24}}</ref> reference book ''[[Dressing a Galaxy: The Costumes of Star Wars]]'' identified the same character as "Seib Nob."<ref name="Dressing">''[[Dressing a Galaxy: The Costumes of Star Wars]]''</ref> Further complicating matters was [[Stephen J. Sansweet]] and [[Pablo Hidalgo]]'s [[October 27]], 2005 reference title ''[[Star Wars Chronicles: The Prequels]]'' identifying the character as "Nob Seib,"<ref name="Chronicles">''[[Star Wars Chronicles: The Prequels]]''</ref> leading continuity editor [[Leland Chee]] to clarify that the original designation, "Seib Nod," is correct.<ref name="Chee">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=158725&start=0#16092867|thread=Spelling Discrepancies|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-12-27|time=6:13 AM}}</ref> The character's story was later fleshed out by [[Captain Yossarian|Benjamin Christopher Norton Dawe]], writing as "Captain Yossarian," through [[StarWars.com]]'s "[[What's The Story?]]" [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] feature.<ref name="db" />
